page: Microsoft Azure F16ads_v7 is a virtual machine instance powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of RAM, translating to 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled, providing 1890 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor.

In benchmark evaluations, the F16ads_v7 demonstrates top-tier single-core performance, placing in the top 10% for stress-ng single-core CPU testing and single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Multi-core CPU performance is strong, ranking in the top 25% for stress-ng multi-core tests. Memory performance is also strong, with PassMark memory scores in the top 25%, while memory bandwidth for both small and large blocks remains in the average range. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the instance achieves top-tier text generation speeds and strong prompt processing speeds on small 135M models, though performance scales down to average on larger 7B and 70B models.

Given its dedicated high-frequency CPU cores and balanced 1:4 memory-to-vCPU ratio, the F16ads_v7 offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for compute-intensive workloads that do not require dedicated GPU acceleration. The inclusion of a substantial 1890 GB local disk provides high value for applications needing fast scratch space. This server is well-suited for workloads such as web serving, single-threaded and multi-threaded data compression, database operations, and lightweight LLM inference tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure F16ads_v7 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz, paired with 64.0 GB of RAM and 1890 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% for stress-ng and single-threaded compression benchmarks. Multi-core capabilities and memory performance are also strong, placing in the top 25% of tested systems. With a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and high-frequency dedicated cores, the F16ads_v7 provides excellent resource density for compute-heavy tasks. It is highly suited for web serving, data compression, database operations, and lightweight language model inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F16ads_v7 is a 16-vCPU virtual machine featuring an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor at 4.5 GHz, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F16ads_v7 features 16 AMD EPYC vCPUs at 4.5 GHz, 64 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, offering top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A high-frequency sixteen-core virtual server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing, balanced memory, and generous local storage for compute-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 vCPUs operating at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz
64.0 GB of system memory providing a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
1890 GB of bundled local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
Strong multi-core execution and lightweight language model inference capabilities
Designed for web serving, database operations, and data compression workloads
